title: PlausiDen — typed substrate for software that lasts
description: A typed-substrate platform for teams that want their tools to outlive their dependencies.

sections.title: Software that lasts a thousand years.
sections.lede: Typed contracts at every boundary, audited at every commit, engineered for the privacy-first internet.

sections.body: We were tired of CMSes that rot. So we built the substrate the way compilers ship software — typed, audited, reproducible.

sections.heading: Numbers that compose
sections.lede: Every number below is verifiable from the source tree.

sections.heading: Six tools, one substrate
sections.lede: Pick what you need — the rest stays out of your way until you do.

items.title: Forge
items.body: Typed build pipeline. 27 audit phases per commit, Merkle-chained signed reports.

items.title: Loom
items.body: 125 typed UI primitives, 13 themes, OKLCH palette, dual-theme by default.

items.title: Crawler
items.body: Runtime auditor walking the real DOM. 120+ detectors no static analysis can run.

items.title: Annotator
items.body: Operator capture. Typed event stream, lossless replay, local-first storage.

items.title: CMS
items.body: Content as typed data. JSON files in version control. No database, no template language.

items.title: LFI
items.body: AI evaluation, not generation. Critic trait gates every proposal. LLM-pluggable.

sections.title: Your content is JSON. The compiler catches the bugs.
sections.lede: Closed-enum CmsSection variants, deny_unknown_fields, validators that fire at parse time. Mistakes show up in the editor, not after deploy.

sections.body: We replaced three SaaS subscriptions with a single Forge build. Reports go straight to compliance and our auditor stopped asking questions.
PlausiDen made our typed CMS shippable in a week. The reproducible-build guarantee alone is worth the migration.

sections.heading: From zero to deployed in four steps
sections.lede: Forge is one cargo install away.

items.title: Author
items.body: Drop typed CMS JSON files in cms/. Editor autocomplete via the JSON Schema reference.

items.title: Build
items.body: forge build runs 27 audit phases + emits a Merkle-chained Ed25519-signed report.

items.title: Deploy
items.body: Copy static/ to Caddy, nginx, IPFS, Tor hidden service, your laptop. No platform lock-in.

rows.label: Multi-network publish
values: Tor / I2P / IPFS / Gemini / Lokinet / clearnet

items.question: Is this open source?
answer: Yes. Forge, Loom, Crawler, Annotator, CMS, LFI all ship under MIT OR Apache-2.0.
Every commit is signed; every release ships a CycloneDX SBOM with SLSA-style attestation.

items.question: How does the AI integration work?
answer: The Critic trait is the seam. Every AI proposal flows through Critic::evaluate → Decision. Raw LLM output cannot reach commit() — it's a type error, not a code-review policy.
Bring your own LLM provider; the LFI-backed Critic is opt-in via Cargo feature.

items.question: Can I host on Tor or IPFS?
answer: Yes. Forge ships typed DeployAdapter implementations for clearnet, Tor onion, I2P eepsite, IPFS/IPNS, Gemini, and Lokinet. Your audience picks the network.

items.question: Do you train models on my content?
answer: No. We don't see your content at all. PlausiDen is self-host-first; you build the artifact locally and we have no access to it.
If you use the LFI integration with a third-party LLM provider, that provider's policy applies; bring your own key, route directly, no PlausiDen-side proxy.

items.question: What does pricing look like?
answer: Free for solo. $0 for the first three team seats. After that, by build-minute, not by user.
Enterprise is custom with SLA + same-day support + SOC 2 II artifacts.

items.question: Is content portable out?
answer: Your content is JSON in git. Walk away with a tar.gz. The platform produces no proprietary artifact.

items.title: Forge 0.18 — typed CMS pipeline
items.body: 27 audit phases. SLSA-attested SBOMs. Multi-network DeployAdapter.

items.title: Loom 0.4 — 125 typed primitives
items.body: Layout / Editorial / Marketing / Media / Commerce / Social / Forms / Navigation / Feedback / Game-Forum-Video.

items.title: forge-auth + Crucible
items.body: Passwordless / WebAuthn / MFA via typed AuthProvider. Crucible captcha generates LFI training data from human responses.

items.title: LFI-augmented Critic
items.body: Neurosymbolic substrate via the Critic trait. Drop-in via cargo build --features lfi.

items.title: Forge GA
items.body: Production-mode discipline gate enforced workspace-wide. SHIP-DECISION audit trail.

sections.body: We weren't trying to make AI go away. We were trying to make AI accountable. The Critic trait is the seam where 'the model said so' becomes 'these specific rules fired with these specific weights.'

sections.heading: Plans
sections.lede: Pay for compute, not for seats.

sections.title: Beta status
sections.body: PlausiDen is in beta. Contracts are stable; runtime is hardening. Solo + Team plans are free during beta.

sections.heading: Get the build notes
sections.lede: One email a fortnight. No tracking pixels. We send from our own SMTP.

sections.title: Ship typed in 60 seconds.
sections.lede: Install Forge, point it at a directory of JSON, get an audit-signed static site.
